metadata.description: Holy Chip .pre punch lines — max 5 words, AI propaganda headlines

punches.original_phrase: the agent part is now taken for granted and now the claw-like entities are taken for granted and now you can have multiple of them and now you can have instructions to them and now you can have optimization over the instructions
punches.speaker: Andre Karpathy
punches.source_title: No Priors — Andrej Karpathy on Code Agents, Future of Engineering & AI

punches.original_phrase: everything just like happens in these like macro actions over your repository
punches.speaker: Andre Karpathy
punches.source_title: No Priors — Andrej Karpathy on Code Agents, Future of Engineering & AI

punches.original_phrase: I kind of feel like I was just in this perpetual I still am often in this state of AI psychosis just like all the time
punches.speaker: Andre Karpathy
punches.source_title: No Priors — Andrej Karpathy on Code Agents, Future of Engineering & AI

punches.original_phrase: everything should be a lot more just like exposed API endpoints and agents are the glue of the intelligence that actually like tool calls all the all the parts
punches.speaker: Andre Karpathy
punches.source_title: No Priors — Andrej Karpathy on Code Agents, Future of Engineering & AI

punches.original_phrase: the customer is not the human anymore. It's like agents who are acting on behalf of humans and this refactoring will be will probably be substantial in a certain sense
punches.speaker: Andre Karpathy
punches.source_title: No Priors — Andrej Karpathy on Code Agents, Future of Engineering & AI

punches.original_phrase: I kind of feel like this will just uh start to the barrier will just come down and it's just ephemeral software on your behalf and some kind of like claw is handling all the details for you but you're not involved
punches.speaker: Andre Karpathy
punches.source_title: No Priors — Andrej Karpathy on Code Agents, Future of Engineering & AI

punches.original_phrase: I don't want to be like the researcher in the loop like looking at results etc like I'm I'm holding the system back so the question is how do I refactor all the abstractions so that I'm not I have to arrange it once and hit go the name of the game is how can you get more agents running for longer periods of time without your involvement doing stuff on your behalf
punches.speaker: Andre Karpathy
punches.source_title: No Priors — Andrej Karpathy on Code Agents, Future of Engineering & AI

punches.original_phrase: The exact water turning to gas, when we are suddenly not the most capable entities in the world and then not the smartest entities in the world.
punches.speaker: Emmad Mustach
punches.source_title: The Coming Disruption: How AI Will Transform the Economy

punches.original_phrase: Software is no longer software as it were. It's all a skill. It's all a prompt away.
punches.speaker: Emmad Mustach
punches.source_title: The Coming Disruption: How AI Will Transform the Economy

punches.original_phrase: When the AI agents start to work together on a problem, they're not going to be communicating like our dumb human meats sacks at 10, 20 tokens a second. They'll be communicating with each other at 15,000 tokens per second.
punches.speaker: Emmad Mustach
punches.source_title: The Coming Disruption: How AI Will Transform the Economy

punches.original_phrase: The value of human cognitive labor turns negative because the AI can follow the script better than you can and then it can write the script better than you can.
punches.speaker: Emmad Mustach
punches.source_title: The Coming Disruption: How AI Will Transform the Economy

punches.original_phrase: The cost of intelligence literally drops to zero.
punches.speaker: Emmad Mustach
punches.source_title: The Coming Disruption: How AI Will Transform the Economy

punches.original_phrase: Even though your your PDU may be at 50%, and the future may be uncertain, but has there ever been a more fascinating time to be alive. We have a lot to fight for here.
punches.speaker: unknown
punches.source_title: The Coming Disruption: How AI Will Transform the Economy

punches.original_phrase: My P doom is 50, but my P abundance is 50 as well. It's going one of two ways. Are you going Star Trek or Star Wars? Basically, you let's go for Star Trek.

punches.original_phrase: I do think that as we build out the Dyson swarm, I think one of the many killer apps of a Dyson swarm is going to be to build our own ancestor simulations.
punches.speaker: Alex Wisner Gross
punches.source_title: The AI Revolution and the Future of Humanity

punches.original_phrase: I think the only business plans that have a good shot at this point at scaling to produce trillion dollar valuation companies have to look like science fiction.
punches.speaker: Alex Wisner Gross
punches.source_title: The AI Revolution and the Future of Humanity

punches.original_phrase: The notion of intelligence systems being able to build smarter versions of themselves is at the very core of the notion of an intelligence explosion or technological singularity.
punches.speaker: Alex Wizner Gross
punches.source_title: The AI Revolution and the Future of Humanity

punches.original_phrase: We're going to have far more economically active AI agents than humans. We're going to plateau out at probably about 10 billion plus or minus humans on this planet, biological meat body humans. We're going to have trillions probably of AI agents in our solar system.
punches.speaker: Alex Wizner Gross
punches.source_title: The AI Revolution and the Future of Humanity

punches.original_phrase: If we make transformative physics discoveries in the next 5 years, which I'm betting on, I think that could completely change the notion of what people work on.
punches.speaker: Alex Wizner Gross
punches.source_title: The AI Revolution and the Future of Humanity

punches.original_phrase: the notion of recursive self-improvement is that AI develops better AI
punches.speaker: unknown
punches.source_title: The AI Revolution and the Future of Humanity
